description:
A Solar Energy Systems Engineer needs to evaluate how different photovoltaic module technologies perform in extreme heat. Model a 50 MW utility-scale plant in Phoenix, Arizona, comparing Standard Crystalline Silicon panels (`module_type=0`) against Thin Film (CdTe) panels (`module_type=2`) using PySAM's `Pvwattsv8` module.

System Specifications:
- System Capacity (DC): 50,000 kW
- DC/AC Ratio: 1.2
- Inverter Efficiency: 96.0 %
- Array Type: Single-axis tracking (`array_type=2`)
- Tilt: 0 degrees
- Azimuth: 180 degrees
- System Losses: 14.08 %
- Ground Coverage Ratio (GCR): 0.4
- Weather Data: Phoenix TMY file (path found in /home/ga/.SAM/solar_resource_dir.txt)

Calculate the percentage energy gain of Thin Film over Standard modules and save the results to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/module_comparison.json following this structure:
{
  "location_used": "<filename or path>",
  "system_capacity_kw": 50000,
  "standard_module": {
    "module_type": 0,
    "annual_energy_kwh": <float>,
    "capacity_factor_percent": <float>
  },
  "thin_film_module": {
    "module_type": 2,
    "annual_energy_kwh": <float>,
    "capacity_factor_percent": <float>
  },
  "thin_film_energy_gain_percent": <float>
}
